commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Slack QA-Lab ping/pong RTT tracing, phase/RSS/heap diagnostics, low-noise canary config, and focused Slack Convex workflow inputs.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows heap snapshots are copied into the uploaded output directory, and a run-wide preservation flag can skip later gateway cleanup. The PR itself is a QA diagnostics feature rather than a user bug report.
commands.review_followup: A narrow repair can remove raw heap snapshots from uploaded artifacts, fix the per-scenario cleanup flag, and add focused coverage before maintainer live proof and merge review.

target.title: test(qa-lab): trace Slack RTT ping pong
target.body:
Summary
- Switch the Slack live canary to a ping/pong marker and measure RTT from Slack accepted timestamps when available.
- Record observer lag, poll counts, gateway RSS samples, gateway phase traces, and optional heap checkpoints for Slack live runs.
- Add focused Slack Convex workflow inputs, low-noise Slack QA config, and a real warmup ping/pong before the measured canary turn.

Baseline
- openclaw-rtt baseline: `runs/slack/2026-05-18T081124268Z-openclaw_main-slack-slack-canary-rtt/result.json` recorded Slack main p50 `4365ms`, p95 `5736ms`, max `8211ms`, max-RSS p50 `742804KB`, p95 `779368KB`, max `874552KB`.
- Git-history pass on the Slack/QA surface showed recent Slack live reliability changes (`7554deef302`, `f0fc8c27d39`, `395346fe57d`) but no existing accepted-timestamp ping/pong RTT or phase/heap checkpoint proof path.

Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/slack/slack-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/shared/live-gateway.runtime.test.ts
- node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/shared/live-gateway.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/slack/slack-live.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/slack/slack-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/slack/src/qa-trace.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts extensions/slack/src/send.ts .github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml CHANGELOG.md
- ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(ARGV.fetch(0)); puts "yaml ok"' .github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml
- /opt/homebrew/bin/actionlint .github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml
- git diff --check
- GitHub Actions run 26027478567, Slack live QA lane with Convex leases, selected SHA 456bcb3a92d4ae4923f6cbde419e19087a7b7bcf

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Slack QA canary RTT was measuring a noisy cold/harness path and gave no phase/RSS/heap visibility for the slow path.
Real environment tested: GitHub Actions Blacksmith runner with Convex Slack credential lease, mock-openai provider mode, Slack canary-only scenario, heap checkpoints enabled. Direct local Crabbox was not used for the secret-bearing run because the installed Crabbox binary failed basic sanity checks; the secret-safe runner used here was the repository Blacksmith/Convex workflow.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: gh workflow run qa-live-transports-convex.yml -R openclaw/openclaw -r perf/slack-rtt-leak-hunt -f ref=456bcb3a92d4ae4923f6cbde419e19087a7b7bcf -f lanes=slack -f slack_scenario=slack-canary -f slack_provider_mode=mock-openai -f slack_heap_checkpoints=true
Evidence after fix: run 26027478567 uploaded qa-live-slack-26027478567-1; Slack canary passed with transport RTT 1638ms, observed RTT 1884ms, observer lag 145ms, 5 channel polls, 0 thread polls, Slack API post 177ms, ready heap 255666286 bytes, reply heap 252435886 bytes.
Observed result after fix: RTT is under the 2000ms target, the report includes "warmed ping/pong dispatch path", and the preserved gateway debug trace shows measured inbound prepare at 3ms before the final Slack post.
What was not tested: pnpm check:changed was not run locally because this is a Codex worktree; broad pnpm-gated proof was routed through the secret-backed GitHub/Blacksmith Slack live lane instead.
target.author: vincentkoc
